title: CHEMICAL ACTIVITY OF HALOGEN DERIVATIVES OF SUBSTITUTED AMIDES OF MALONIC ACID. PART I. INTERACTION OF GRIGNARD'S REAGENT ON THE CHLORO DERIVATIVES OF SUBSTITUTED AMIDES OF MALONIC ACID
author: K. G. NAIK
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The following dichloro derivatives of the substituted amides of anionic add have been interacted with the Griguard's reagent viz. phenyl magnesium bromide and bawl magnesium chloride. 

 (I) Dichloromalondiphenylamide. (a) Dichlaromalondi- p -tolylamide. (3) Dichloromalondi- o -tolylamide (4) Dichloromalondi-1 : 3 : 4-xylylamide. 

 Contrary to expectations, instead of ditertiary alcohols, monochloro derivatives of the following type were obtained, from the above substances  viz.  

   ( C1)HC   : (CONHR) 2 , where R may be phenyl, tolyl or xylyl group.
steps_raw:
The following dichloro derivatives of the substituted amides of anionic add have been interacted with the Griguard's reagent viz. phenyl magnesium bromide and bawl magnesium chloride. 

 (I) Dichloromalondiphenylamide. (a) Dichlaromalondi- p -tolylamide. (3) Dichloromalondi- o -tolylamide (4) Dichloromalondi-1 : 3 : 4-xylylamide. 

 Contrary to expectations, instead of ditertiary alcohols, monochloro derivatives of the following type were obtained, from the above substances  viz.  

   ( C1)HC   : (CONHR) 2 , where R may be phenyl, tolyl or xylyl group.
